heading: Credit for producing fuel from a nonconventional source
label: 45K Credit for producing fuel from a nonconventional source
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain Tax YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Internal Revenue Notices listed in a table below.

L. 101–508, § 11816(b)(1)“(A) .—The term ‘qualifying processed wood fuel’ means any processed solid wood fuel (other than charcoal, fireplace products, or a product used for ornamental or recreational purposes) which has a Btu content per unit of volume or weight, determined without regard to any nonwood elements, which is at least 40 percent greater per unit of volume or weight than the Btu content of the wood from which it is produced (determined immediately before the processing).In general“(B) .—A taxpayer shall elect, at such time and in such manner as the Secretary by regulations may prescribe, as to whether Btu content per unit shall be determined for purposes of this paragraph on a volume or weight basis. children.text: $3, multiplied by

children.label: A
children.text: sold by the taxpayer to an unrelated person during the taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the production of which is attributable to the taxpayer.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount by which the reference price for the calendar year in which the sale occurs exceeds $23.50, bears to

children.heading: Credit and phaseout adjustment based on inflation
children.label: 2 Credit and phaseout adjustment based on inflation
children.text: The $3 amount in subsection (a) and the $23.50 and $6 amounts in paragraph (1) shall each be adjusted by multiplying such amount by the inflation adjustment factor for the calendar year in which the sale occurs. In the case of gas from a tight formation, the $3 amount in subsection (a) shall not be adjusted.

children.heading: Credit reduced for grants, tax-exempt bonds, and subsidized energy financing
children.label: 3 Credit reduced for grants, tax-exempt bonds, and subsidized energy financing
I
children.text: grants provided by the United States, a State, or a political subdivision of a State for use in connection with the project,

children.label: II
children.text: proceeds of any issue of State or local government obligations used to provide financing for the project the interest on which is exempt from tax under section 103, and

children.label: III
children.text: the aggregate amount of subsidized energy financing (within the meaning of section 48(a)(4)(C)) provided in connection with the project, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the denominator of which is the aggregate amount of additions to the capital account for the project for the taxable year and all prior taxable years.

children.heading: Amounts determined at close of year
children.label: B Amounts determined at close of year
children.text: The amounts under subparagraph (A) for any taxable year shall be determined as of the close of the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate amount allowed under section 38 for the taxable year or any prior taxable year by reason of the energy percentage with respect to property used in the project, over

children.label: i
children.text: under section 49(b) or 50(a) for the taxable year or any prior taxable year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: under this paragraph for any prior taxable year.

children.heading: Credit reduced for enhanced oil recovery credit
children.label: 5 Credit reduced for enhanced oil recovery credit
A
children.text: the aggregate amount allowed under section 38 for the taxable year and any prior taxable year by reason of any enhanced oil recovery credit determined under section 43 with respect to such project, over

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount recaptured with respect to the amount described in subparagraph (A) under this paragraph for any prior taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: oil produced from shale and tar sands,

children.label: i
children.text: geopressured brine, Devonian shale, coal seams, or a tight formation, or

children.label: ii
children.text: biomass, and

children.label: C
children.text: liquid, gaseous, or solid synthetic fuels produced from coal (including lignite), including such fuels when used as feedstocks.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the determination of whether any gas is produced from geopressured brine, Devonian shale, coal seams, or a tight formation shall be made in accordance with section 503 of the Natural Gas Policy Act of 1978 (as in effect before the repeal of such section).

children.heading: Special rules for gas from tight formations
children.label: B Special rules for gas from tight formations
i
children.text: which, as of , was committed or dedicated to interstate commerce (as defined in section 2(18) of the Natural Gas Policy Act of 1978, as in effect on the date of the enactment of this clause), orApril 20, 1977

children.label: ii
children.text: which is produced from a well drilled after such date of enactment.

children.label: A
children.text: oil and natural gas (or any product thereof), and

children.label: B
children.text: coal (including lignite) or any product thereof.

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: d Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Only production within the United States taken into account
children.label: 1 Only production within the United States taken into account
A
children.text: the United States (within the meaning of section 638(1)), or

children.label: B
children.text: a possession of the United States (within the meaning of section 638(2)).

children.heading: Computation of inflation adjustment factor and reference price
children.label: 2 Computation of inflation adjustment factor and reference price

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, not later than April 1 of each calendar year, determine and publish in the Federal Register the inflation adjustment factor and the reference price for the preceding calendar year in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: Inflation adjustment factor
children.label: B Inflation adjustment factor
children.text: The term “inflation adjustment factor” means, with respect to a calendar year, a fraction the numerator of which is the GNP implicit price deflator for the calendar year and the denominator of which is the GNP implicit price deflator for calendar year 1979. The term “GNP implicit price deflator” means the first revision of the implicit price deflator for the gross national product as computed and published by the Department of Commerce.

children.heading: Reference price
children.label: C Reference price
children.text: The term “reference price” means with respect to a calendar year the Secretary’s estimate of the annual average wellhead price per barrel for all domestic crude oil the price of which is not subject to regulation by the United States.

children.heading: Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.label: 3 Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.text: In the case of a property or facility in which more than 1 person has an interest, except to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, production from the property or facility (as the case may be) shall be allocated among such persons in proportion to their respective interests in the gross sales from such property or facility.

children.heading: Gas from geopressured brine, Devonian shale, coal seams, or a tight formation
children.label: 4 Gas from geopressured brine, Devonian shale, coal seams, or a tight formation
children.text: The amount of the credit allowable under subsection (a) shall be determined without regard to any production attributable to a property from which gas from Devonian shale, coal seams, geopressured brine, or a tight formation was produced in marketable quantities before .January 1, 1980

children.heading: Barrel-of-oil equivalent
children.label: 5 Barrel-of-oil equivalent
children.text: The term “barrel-of-oil equivalent” with respect to any fuel means that amount of such fuel which has a Btu content of 5.8 million; except that in the case of qualified fuels described in subparagraph (C) of subsection (c)(1), the Btu content shall be determined without regard to any material from a source not described in such subparagraph.

children.heading: Barrel defined
children.label: 6 Barrel defined
children.text: The term “barrel” means 42 United States gallons.

children.heading: Related persons
children.label: 7 Related persons
children.text: Persons shall be treated as related to each other if such persons would be treated as a single employer under the regulations prescribed under section 52(b). In the case of a corporation which is a member of an affiliated group of corporations filing a consolidated return, such corporation shall be treated as selling qualified fuels to an unrelated person if such fuels are sold to such a person by another member of such group.

children.heading: Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.label: 8 Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, rules similar to the rules of subsection (d) of section 52 shall apply.

children.label: A
children.text: produced from a well drilled after , and before , orDecember 31, 1979January 1, 1993

children.label: B
children.text: produced in a facility placed in service after , and before , andDecember 31, 1979January 1, 1993
which are sold before .January 1, 2003

children.label: A
children.text: for purposes of subsection (e)(1)(B), such facility shall be treated as being placed in service before , if such facility is placed in service before , pursuant to a binding written contract in effect before , andJanuary 1, 1993July 1, 1998January 1, 1997

children.label: B
children.text: if such facility is originally placed in service after , paragraph (2) of subsection (e) shall be applied with respect to such facility by substituting “” for “”.December 31, 1992January 1, 2008January 1, 2003

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 2 Special rule
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any facility which produces coke or coke gas unless the original use of the facility commences with the taxpayer.

children.heading: Extension for facilities producing coke or coke gas
children.label: g Extension for facilities producing coke or coke gas
A
children.text: beginning on the later of , or the date that such facility is placed in service, andJanuary 1, 2006

children.label: B
children.text: ending on the date which is 4 years after the date such period began.

children.heading: Daily limit
children.label: A Daily limit
children.text: The amount of qualified fuels sold during any taxable year which may be taken into account by reason of this subsection with respect to any facility shall not exceed an average barrel-of-oil equivalent of 4,000 barrels per day. Days before the date the facility is placed in service shall not be taken into account in determining such average.

children.heading: Extension period to commence with unadjusted credit amount
children.label: B Extension period to commence with unadjusted credit amount
children.text: For purposes of applying subsection (b)(2) to the $3 amount in subsection (a), in the case of fuels sold after 2005, subsection (d)(2)(B) shall be applied by substituting “2004” for “1979”.

children.heading: Denial of double benefit
children.label: C Denial of double benefit
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any facility producing qualified fuels for which a credit was allowed under this section for the taxable year or any preceding taxable year by reason of subsection (f).

children.heading: Nonapplication of phaseout
children.label: D Nonapplication of phaseout
children.text: Subsection (b)(1) shall not apply.

children.heading: Coordination with section 45
children.label: E Coordination with section 45
children.text: No credit shall be allowed with respect to any coke or coke gas which is produced using steel industry fuel (as defined in section 45(c)(7)) as feedstock if a credit is allowed to any taxpayer under section 45 with respect to the production of such steel industry fuel.
